purpose: Reusable entry/exit style menu for testing regular and creative trade lifecycle logic across pair strategies.

signal_convention.long_spread: signal > 0: long asset Y and short asset X
signal_convention.short_spread: signal < 0: short asset Y and long asset X

strategy.description: The base strategy or signal family being tested, such as zscore, copula, ECM, Ghost Ambush, or portfolio ranking.

entry_style.description: The condition or lifecycle pattern that opens a position.

exit_style.description: The rule that closes, scales down, reverses, or invalidates a position.

regime_filter.description: A market-state gate that decides whether the signal is allowed.

regime_behavior.description: Whether the regime filter applies only to entries or also forces exits.

regime_filters.purpose: Check whether dislocations repair better in rising markets.
Check whether downside regimes create useful spread repair or only drawdown.
Trade only when spread behavior is calmer.
Only trade when the hedge relationship is stable.
Avoid the two states most likely to widen drawdown.

regime_behaviors.description: Use the regime filter only to allow or block new entries. Existing trades exit through their normal lifecycle.
Use the regime filter for entry, and close immediately when the regime becomes hostile after entry.

regular.name: Fixed Threshold
regular.entry: Enter when the base signal crosses a fixed entry threshold.
regular.exit: Exit when the base signal returns to a fixed neutral threshold.
regular.stop: Optional fixed stop if the signal moves beyond a maximum adverse threshold.

regular.name: Signal Flip
regular.entry: Enter long or short when the base signal points in that direction.
regular.exit: Exit and reverse when the opposite signal appears.
regular.stop: Optional drawdown or volatility stop to avoid churn.

regular.name: Mean Reversion Exit
regular.entry: Enter when spread, z-score, or copula distortion is dislocated.
regular.exit: Exit when the dislocation returns near fair value.
regular.stop: Stop if the dislocation expands by a defined volatility multiple.

regular.name: Time Stop
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit after a maximum number of bars if the trade has not resolved.

regular.name: Stop Loss
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit if open trade loss exceeds a fixed limit.
regular.stop: Fixed adverse PnL, spread, or z-score limit.

regular.name: Take Profit
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit when target profit is reached.

regular.name: Trailing Stop
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit when a profitable trade gives back more than the allowed amount.
regular.stop: Trailing giveback from best open PnL.

regular.name: Partial Exit
regular.entry: Enter full size when the setup triggers.
regular.exit: Reduce size at multiple target milestones.
regular.stop: Remaining size exits on neutral signal, time stop, or loss stop.

regular.name: Volatility Stop
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit if the spread moves against the trade by N recent volatility units.

regular.name: Max Drawdown Stop
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: Exit if trade-level drawdown exceeds the limit.

regular.name: Break-Even Stop
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal.
regular.exit: After the trade reaches a profit trigger, move stop to break-even.

regular.name: Risk Reward Target
regular.entry: Enter on the normal strategy signal with a defined risk unit.
regular.exit: Exit at a fixed reward-to-risk multiple, such as 2R.

regular.name: Scale In
regular.entry: Add position as signal strength increases or confirmation improves.
regular.exit: Exit normally or reduce when signal weakens.
regular.stop: Stop if maximum planned size is reached and signal still deteriorates.

regular.name: Scale Out
regular.entry: Enter full or planned size on the base signal.
regular.exit: Reduce gradually as target is approached.
regular.stop: Final remainder exits on neutral, time stop, or drawdown stop.

regular.name: Regime Exit
regular.entry: Enter on the normal signal only when regime allows trading.
regular.exit: Exit if market regime changes to a hostile state.

regular.name: Funding Exit
regular.entry: Enter only when funding is helpful or small relative to expected edge.
regular.exit: Exit if funding flips hostile or exceeds expected edge.

regular.name: Liquidity Exit
regular.entry: Enter only when liquidity, spread, and slippage are acceptable.
regular.exit: Exit early if liquidity becomes too thin.

creative.name: Rubber Band Snap
creative.entry: Enter when the spread stretches far from fair value and the last bar shows exhaustion.
creative.exit: Exit when the spread snaps back to the inner neutral band.
creative.danger: Bad when the stretch is actually the start of a trend.

creative.name: Second Knock
creative.entry: Wait for the signal to hit the extreme, pull back, then fail again at the same extreme.
creative.exit: Exit partial size at midpoint and final size at mean.

creative.name: Trap Door Fade
creative.entry: Let the signal break the extreme level, then enter only when it falls back inside.
creative.exit: Exit at the old centerline or after a fixed snapback window.
creative.danger: Gets hurt if the breakout is real.

creative.name: Pressure Cooker
creative.entry: Enter only after dislocation stays extreme for several bars without accelerating.
creative.exit: Exit when pressure releases below the persistence band.

creative.name: First Crack
creative.entry: Enter when the extreme signal makes its first lower high or higher low.
creative.exit: Exit when half the dislocation is repaired.

creative.name: Tide Turn
creative.entry: Enter only when regime shifts from hostile or trending into range or calm while spread is still stretched.
creative.exit: Exit when regime turns hostile again.

creative.name: Magnet Pull
creative.entry: Enter when the distance to fair value is large and expected pull exceeds fees and funding.
creative.exit: Exit at fair value or when expected pull drops below cost.

creative.name: Vanishing Edge
creative.entry: Enter when signal strength and confirmation both pass.
creative.exit: Exit the moment either the signal or confirmation falls below maintenance threshold.
creative.danger: May exit too early in noisy chop.

creative.name: Three-Key Vault
creative.entry: Enter only when spread, probability, and correction-force signals all agree.
creative.exit: Exit when two keys fail.

creative.name: Two-Key Scout
creative.entry: Enter when any two of three signals agree, but use smaller size.
creative.exit: Exit when only one signal remains.

creative.name: Signal Parliament
creative.entry: Each model gets a vote weighted by recent reliability; enter when the vote clears quorum.
creative.exit: Exit when quorum breaks.
creative.danger: Overreacts if reliability weights are unstable.

creative.name: King Of The Hill
creative.entry: Enter only if this pair is ranked near the top of the whole universe.
creative.exit: Exit when another pair takes the crown.
creative.danger: Can churn capital if ranks are noisy.

creative.name: Falling Crown
creative.entry: Enter when pair breaks into the top-rank bucket.
creative.exit: Exit only after it falls two buckets, not one.

creative.name: Shark Fin
creative.entry: Enter when signal spikes sharply then immediately curls back.
creative.exit: Exit fast at partial normalization.
creative.danger: Designed for quick trades, not slow reversion.

creative.name: Iceberg Melt
creative.entry: Enter as dislocation gradually compresses from an extreme, not on the extreme itself.
creative.exit: Exit in stages as compression milestones are reached.
creative.danger: Needs patience and good time stops.

creative.name: Coiled Spring
creative.entry: Wait for volatility to compress while spread remains stretched, then enter on first move toward mean.
creative.exit: Exit when volatility expands or mean is reached.
creative.danger: Spring can release the wrong way.

creative.name: Circuit Breaker
creative.entry: Normal entry rules are allowed only if tail risk, liquidity, and funding are green.
creative.exit: Exit immediately if any risk circuit trips.

creative.name: Storm Window
creative.entry: After a volatility shock, wait for stress to cool before entering.
creative.exit: Exit quickly; no heroic holds.
creative.danger: Can miss the exact bottom or top.

creative.name: Smoke Alarm
creative.entry: Enter normal mean-reversion trades only when tail dependence is low.
creative.exit: Exit if tail dependence rises faster than normal.

creative.name: Funding Wind
creative.entry: Enter only when funding helps or is small relative to expected edge.
creative.exit: Exit if funding flips against the position.

creative.name: Time Fuse
creative.entry: Enter normally.
creative.exit: Exit after N bars if the trade has not worked.
creative.danger: May close right before delayed reversion.

creative.name: Half-Life Fuse
creative.entry: Enter only if expected half-life is tradable.
creative.exit: Exit after 1-2 half-lives if not resolved.

creative.name: Profit Ladder
creative.entry: Enter full or scaled size.
creative.exit: Take partial profits at 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100% mean reversion.
creative.danger: Can reduce profits on clean full reversions.

creative.name: Moonwalk Exit
creative.entry: Enter on dislocation.
creative.exit: Trail exit behind the moving fair value instead of using a fixed mean.

creative.name: Stop And Flip
creative.entry: Enter long or short based on signal direction.
creative.exit: Exit current side and reverse when opposite signal triggers.

creative.name: Ghost Trade
creative.entry: When signal fires, shadow trade first; enter real only if first few bars behave correctly.
creative.exit: Exit if the shadow profile breaks.

creative.name: Ambush Limit
creative.entry: Signal creates a limit order at a better spread level; trade only if price comes to you.
creative.exit: Exit with passive orders near target.

creative.name: Liquidity Tide
creative.entry: Enter only when order book depth and spread cost are favorable.
creative.exit: Exit early if liquidity thins.

creative.name: Drawdown Ratchet
creative.entry: Enter normally.
creative.exit: Stop gets tighter each bar the trade fails to improve.

creative.name: Winner's Leash
creative.entry: Enter normally.
creative.exit: Once profitable, trail by giveback amount rather than fixed target.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Fixed Threshold + Mean Reversion Exit
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Baseline lifecycle for comparing every creative style.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Fixed Threshold + Time Stop
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Checks whether BTC-DOGE edge dies when trades are not allowed to linger.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Mean Reversion Exit + Volatility Stop
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Adds a spread-volatility invalidation stop to the plain reversion setup.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Iceberg Melt + Profit Ladder
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Favorite first creative test: enter after compression starts and harvest reversion in pieces.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Trap Door Fade + Time Fuse
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Fades false breaks while refusing to sit in dead trades.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: King Of The Hill + Smoke Alarm
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Only trades top dislocations and exits if tail risk wakes up.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Three-Key Vault + Vanishing Edge
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: High-conviction entry with strict confirmation decay exit.

first_batch_for_btc_doge.label: Ghost Trade + Ambush Limit
first_batch_for_btc_doge.why: Lets the first move prove itself, then demands a better execution price.

test_expansion_rule.description: Any strategy/style test can be expanded across regime overlays.
